title: Aisle Seven
logline: A pair of earbuds and a fogged mirror put a picker one step from a five-ton forklift.

beats.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE, AISLE SEVEN - DAWN
beats.description: Dawn shift. Racks tower over faded floor tape. Dana clips on a hi-vis vest, slides in earbuds, and heads for aisle seven while a forklift works the far end, reversing toward the blind corner under a fogged convex mirror.
beats.vo: Tuesday, 6:41 a.m. Third near-miss this quarter starts like every other shift.

beats.heading: INT. AISLE SEVEN, BLIND CORNER - MOMENTS LATER
beats.description: The forklift emerges from the blind corner exactly as Dana, earbuds in, steps off the pedestrian lane. The operator slams the brake; the pallet load shifts; the forks stop a hand's width from Dana's hip. Both freeze in the silence.
beats.vo: He never heard it coming - and the mirror was right there.

beats.heading: INT. AISLE SEVEN - LATER
beats.description: The supervisor files the incident report. Replay from the convex mirror: both paths visible the whole time. Corrective pass: earbuds out, the marked pedestrian lane walked, the mirror wiped clean.
beats.vo: The next one is not a near-miss unless somebody changes the routine today.

rule_card: Earbuds out on the floor. Walk the marked lane. Mirrors are for looking.
